topics: 🩹 ריפוי: complications from a wound — when does the מזיק still pay?
🥒 שבת: 'as if he were a שומר קישואין' — two kinds of שבת
😳 בושת: only when intentional — וְשָׁלְחָה יָדָהּ
👁️ Two simultaneous injuries to a slave — does he go free?
🤲 R' Yochanan's mercy: rule of law vs. rule of compassion

overview.title: 📖 Overview — Working Through the Five Payments
overview.label: The sugya:
overview.text: The daf continues the chapter on bodily damage, walking through ריפוי (medical costs), שבת (loss of earnings), and בושת (embarrassment). R' Yirmiyah asks about complications from a wound, R' Yitzchak distinguishes two types of שבת, and the sugya explores intent in בושת via the pasuk וְשָׁלְחָה יָדָהּ. The chapter then turns to slaves, contrasting the strict halakhic rule with R' Yochanan's personal practice of feeding his slave from his own meat and wine.
overview.hebrewSource: רִבִּי יִרְמְיָה בָעֵי. עָלוּ בוֹ צְמָחִין סְבִיבוֹת הַמַּכָּה וְנִסְתְּרָה מַחֲמַת הַמַּכָּה מָהוּ. מִן מַה דִכְתִיב וְרַפֹּא יְרַפֵּא

slides.title: 🩹 R' Yirmiyah's Question About Complications
slides.subtitle: What if growths form around the wound and complications follow?

nodes.content:
עָלוּ בוֹ צְמָחִין סְבִיבוֹת הַמַּכָּה
Growths form around the wound
Source: וְרַפֹּא יְרַפֵּא — and surely heal

columns.label: Patient ignored doctor
columns.content:
עָבַר עַל דְּבַר רוֹפֵא
מזיק is פטור

nodes.content: R' Shimon: חִידּוּשׁ חִידֵּשׁ הַכָּתוּב — Torah innovated that שבת and ריפוי go together

callout.label: Key move:
callout.text: The Yerushalmi reads רק שבתו יתן ורפא ירפא as a single legislative gesture: שבת and ריפוי are paired.

slides.title: 🥒 Two Kinds of שבת
slides.subtitle: R' Yitzchak resolves a tension in our Mishnah.

boxes.title: 🥒 Permanent loss
items: Hand cut off — already paid נזק for the limb
רוֹאִין אוֹתוֹ כִּילּוּ חִיגֵּר יוֹשֵׁב וְשׁוֹמֵר קִישּׁוּאִין
Calculated only at the watchman wage

boxes.title: 💼 Temporary disability
items: Burn, swelling, exposure to snow / cold water
Was earning a מנה? pays a מנה. סלע? pays a סלע

boxes.title: 🩹 ריפוי still applies
items: בְּמָקוֹם שֶׁאֵינוֹ נִרְאֶה — even hidden injury
Snow / cold water count as injury

callout.label: R' Yitzchak's resolution:
callout.text: תְּרֵין שִׁבְתִּין אִינּוּן — there are two distinct שבת frameworks: one for permanent loss already compensated, one for temporary disability still earning a wage.

slides.title: 💬 בושת and Intent
slides.subtitle: וְשָׁלְחָה יָדָהּ — does the wife of the בית דין marshal count?

lines.speaker: M
lines.text: Mishnah: נָפַל מִן הַגַּג וְהִזִּיק וּבִיֵישׁ — חַיָיב עַל הַנֶּזֶק וּפָטוּר עַל הַבּוֹשֶׁת — בושת requires intent.

lines.speaker: T1
lines.text: וְשָׁלְחָה יָדָהּ — but not the wife of a בית דין marshal (excluded).

lines.speaker: T2
lines.text: Another tanna: לְרַבּוֹת אֵשֶׁת שְׁלִיחַ בֵּית דִּין — including her!

lines.speaker: Y
lines.text: Resolution: excluded when he whipped with permission; included when he whipped without permission.

callout.label: Practical reading:
callout.text: Authorized court-ordered injury creates no claim for the marshal's wife; rogue violence does. The same pasuk is read both ways depending on context.

slides.title: 👁️ Witnesses Testifying About Two Injuries to a Slave
slides.subtitle: The baraita on simultaneous vs. sequential blinding/tooth-knocking.
rows: שְׁתֵּי עֵינָיו כְּאַחַת — both eyes simultaneously
Goes free (per Torah law for an eye)
אֵינוֹ נוֹתֵן לוֹ כְּלוּם — pays nothing
Pays full דְּמֵי שְׁנִיָיה for the second
שָׁמִין לַעֲבָדִים בּוֹשֶׁת — slaves do get embarrassment
Against R' Yehuda's view in the Mishnah

callout.label: Why it matters:
callout.text: Once the slave has gone free, the second injury is to a free person — so it must be paid in full to him, not to the former master.

slides.title: 🤲 R' Yochanan: Law vs. Mercy
slides.subtitle: A halakhic ruling and a personal practice in tension.

steps.content: 1️⃣ Halakhah: הַקּוֹטֵעַ יְדֵי עֶבֶד חֲבֵירוֹ — master collects all five payments.
2️⃣ The amputated slave is supported מִן הַצְּדָקָה — by communal welfare.
3️⃣ R' Acha: Israel is more obligated to support free people than slaves.
4️⃣ But: רִבִּי יוֹחָנָן אֲכִיל קוֹפַּד וִיהִיב לְעַבְדֵּיהּ — R' Yochanan ate meat and gave to his slave.
5️⃣ Resolution: תַּמָּן בְּמִידַּת הַדִּין. בְּרַם הָכָא בְּמִידַּת רַחֲמִים — there a rule of law, here a rule of mercy.

callout.label: Bottom line:
callout.text: R' Yochanan recited הֲלֹא בְּבֶטֶן עוֹשֵׂינִי עֹשֵׂהוּ — 'did not the same Maker form us in the womb?' Halakhah sets a floor; piety builds higher.

summary.principle: The five payments unfold case by case: ריפוי includes complications, שבת comes in two flavors, בושת requires intent, and slave-injury splits the strict law from the merciful practice.

flowSteps.content:
Mishnah continues
ריפוי, שבת, בושת — and the slave cases
Yerushalmi presses each payment for its limits

columns.content:
🥒 שבת
two kinds: שומר קישואין or full wage

flowSteps.content:
🎓 Key Principle:
Each payment has its own contour. R' Yochanan's life shows the gap between what the law demands and what mercy invites.

quiz.q: What does R' Yirmiyah ask regarding complications from a wound?
o: Whether the מזיק must pay if the patient dies
Whether growths around the wound (צְמָחִין סְבִיבוֹת הַמַּכָּה) keep the מזיק liable
Whether bandages count as ריפוי
Whether the doctor must be paid in advance
quiz.e: R' Yirmiyah asks about עָלוּ בוֹ צְמָחִין סְבִיבוֹת הַמַּכָּה וְנִסְתְּרָה מַחֲמַת הַמַּכָּה מָהוּ — the source וְרַפֹּא יְרַפֵּא is invoked.

quiz.q: What does R' Yitzchak mean by תְּרֵין שִׁבְתִּין אִינּוּן?
o: There are two days of work each week
There are two distinct frameworks of שבת — permanent (שומר קישואין) and temporary (full wage)
There are two witnesses required for שבת
Two coins must be paid for each day of שבת
quiz.e: He distinguishes the case of an amputated limb (already paid) from temporary disability where one pays the actual lost wage.

quiz.q: Why does the Mishnah exempt one who fell from the roof from בושת?
o: Because falling is unavoidable
Because אֵינוֹ חַיָיב עַל הַבּוֹשֶׁת עַד שֶׁיְּהֵא מִתְכַּוֵין — בושת requires intent
Because there were no witnesses
Because the falling person is exempt from all damages
quiz.e: The Mishnah derives intent for בושת from וְשָׁלְחָה יָדָהּ וְהֶחֱזִיקָה בִּמְבוּשָׁיו.

quiz.q: If witnesses testify that someone blinded both of a slave's eyes simultaneously, what is the law?
o: The slave goes free and is paid for one eye
The slave goes free and is paid for both eyes
The slave goes free, but the מזיק pays nothing
The slave does not go free
quiz.e: סִימָּא שְׁתֵּי עֵינָיו כְּאַחַת... אֵינוֹ נוֹתֵן לוֹ כְּלוּם. Sequential blinding is different — first eye frees him, second eye must be paid in full.

quiz.q: How does the Yerushalmi reconcile R' Yochanan's halakhic ruling with his personal generosity to his slave?
o: R' Yochanan changed his mind
תַּמָּן בְּמִידַּת הַדִּין. בְּרַם הָכָא בְּמִידַּת רַחֲמִים — strict law there, mercy here
His slave was actually a free man
He was wealthy and could afford it
quiz.e: R' Yochanan recited הֲלֹא בְּבֶטֶן עוֹשֵׂינִי עֹשֵׂהוּ. The halakhah sets a floor; the rule of mercy goes beyond it.
